req.title: Feature flag rollout
req.statement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment; toggles shall be audit-logged.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for feature flag toggle functionality with audit logging.
Requirement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment;
             toggles shall be audit-logged.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake domain models (replace with real imports in production)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionError(Exception):
    pass


class EnvironmentNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class FeatureFlagNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class FeatureFlag:
    def __init__(self, name: str, enabled: bool = False):
        self.name = name
        self.enabled = enabled

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover
        return f"FeatureFlag(name={self.name!r}, enabled={self.enabled})"


class AuditLogEntry:
    def __init__(
        self,
        actor: str,
        action: str,
        flag_name: str,
        environment: str,
        previous_value: bool,
        new_value: bool,
        timestamp: datetime,
    ):
        self.actor = actor
        self.action = action
        self.flag_name = flag_name
        self.environment = environment
        self.previous_value = previous_value
        self.new_value = new_value
        self.timestamp = timestamp


class FeatureFlagService:
    """
    Service under test.  Uses injected repository and audit-logger so both
    can be replaced with test doubles.
    """

    VALID_ENVIRONMENTS = {"development", "staging", "production"}

    def __init__(self, repository: Any, audit_logger: Any, auth_service: Any):
        self._repo = repository
        self._audit = audit_logger
        self._auth = auth_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def toggle(
        self,
        *,
        actor: str,
        flag_name: str,
        environment: str,
        enabled: bool,
    ) -> FeatureFlag:
        # 1. Authorisation
        if not self._auth.is_admin(actor):
            raise PermissionError(f"{actor!r} is not an admin")

        # 2. Validate environment
        if environment not in self.VALID_ENVIRONMENTS:
            raise EnvironmentNotFoundError(f"Unknown environment: {environment!r}")

        # 3. Load existing flag (raises FeatureFlagNotFoundError if absent)
        flag = self._repo.get(flag_name=flag_name, environment=environment)

        previous_value = flag.enabled

        # 4. Persist the change
        flag.enabled = enabled
        self._repo.save(flag=flag, environment=environment)

        # 5. Audit log – MUST happen after a successful save
        self._audit.log(
            AuditLogEntry(
                actor=actor,
                action="TOGGLE",
                flag_name=flag_name,
                environment=environment,
                previous_value=previous_value,
                new_value=enabled,
                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            )
        )

        return flag


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_repo():
    return MagicMock(name="feature_flag_repository")


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_audit():
    return MagicMock(name="audit_logger")


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_auth():
    auth = MagicMock(name="auth_service")
    auth.is_admin.return_value = True          # default: actor IS an admin
    return auth


@pytest.fixture()
def service(mock_repo, mock_audit, mock_auth):
    return FeatureFlagService(
        repository=mock_repo,
        audit_logger=mock_audit,
        auth_service=mock_auth,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def existing_flag():
    """A flag that is currently disabled."""
    return FeatureFlag(name="dark_mode", enabled=False)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit) -> AuditLogEntry:
    """Return the AuditLogEntry that was passed to audit_logger.log()."""
    assert mock_audit.log.called, "audit_logger.log() was never called"
    return mock_audit.log.call_args[0][0]


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY-PATH: ADMIN CAN TOGGLE FLAGS PER ENVIRONMENT
# ===========================================================================

class TestAdminToggleHappyPath:

    def test_enable_flag_returns_updated_flag(self, service, mock_repo, existing_flag):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        result = service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        assert result.enabled is True

    def test_disable_flag_returns_updated_flag(self, service, mock_repo):
        flag = FeatureFlag(name="dark_mode", enabled=True)
        mock_repo.get.return_value = flag

        result = service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="staging",
            enabled=False,
        )

        assert result.enabled is False

    def test_repository_save_called_with_correct_arguments(
        self, service, mock_repo, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="staging",
            enabled=True,
        )

        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once_with(
            flag=existing_flag, environment="staging"
        )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("environment", ["development", "staging", "production"])
    def test_toggle_works_for_every_valid_environment(
        self, service, mock_repo, environment
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = FeatureFlag("beta_feature", enabled=False)

        result = service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="beta_feature",
            environment=environment,
            enabled=True,
        )

        assert result.enabled is True
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_idempotent_enable_already_enabled_flag(self, service, mock_repo):
        """Toggling to True when already True is still valid and must be logged."""
        flag = FeatureFlag("beta_feature", enabled=True)
        mock_repo.get.return_value = flag

        result = service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="beta_feature",
            environment="development",
            enabled=True,
        )

        assert result.enabled is True
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_repository_lookup_uses_correct_flag_and_environment(
        self, service, mock_repo, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        mock_repo.get.assert_called_once_with(
            flag_name="dark_mode", environment="production"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. AUDIT LOGGING
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditLogging:

    def test_audit_log_called_exactly_once_on_successful_toggle(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_entry_records_correct_actor(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.actor == "admin@example.com"

    def test_audit_entry_records_correct_flag_name(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.flag_name == "dark_mode"

    def test_audit_entry_records_correct_environment(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="staging",
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.environment == "staging"

    def test_audit_entry_records_previous_value_as_false(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        """existing_flag starts disabled (False)."""
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.previous_value is False

    def test_audit_entry_records_previous_value_as_true(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit
    ):
        flag = FeatureFlag("dark_mode", enabled=True)
        mock_repo.get.return_value = flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=False,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.previous_value is True

    def test_audit_entry_records_new_value_true(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.new_value is True

    def test_audit_entry_records_new_value_false(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit
    ):
        flag = FeatureFlag("dark_mode", enabled=True)
        mock_repo.get.return_value = flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=False,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.new_value is False

    def test_audit_entry_action_is_toggle(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.action == "TOGGLE"

    def test_audit_entry_has_utc_timestamp(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag
        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)

        assert entry.timestamp.tzinfo == timezone.utc
        assert before <= entry.timestamp <= after

    def test_audit_logged_after_successful_save(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        """Verify ordering: save must precede audit log."""
        call_order = []
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda **_: call_order.append("save")
        mock_audit.log.side_effect = lambda _: call_order.append("audit")

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        assert call_order == ["save", "audit"]

    def test_audit_not_logged_when_save_raises(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, existing_flag
    ):
        """If persistence fails the audit entry must NOT be written."""
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = IOError("DB unavailable")

        with pytest.raises(IOError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_audit.log.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("environment", ["development", "staging", "production"])
    def test_each_environment_toggle_produces_separate_audit_entry(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, environment
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = FeatureFlag("feature_x", enabled=False)

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="feature_x",
            environment=environment,
            enabled=True,
        )

        entry = _captured_audit_entry(mock_audit)
        assert entry.environment == environment


# ===========================================================================
# 3. AUTHORISATION – only admins may toggle
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthorisation:

    def test_non_admin_cannot_toggle_flag(self, service, mock_auth):
        mock_auth.is_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="user@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

    def test_non_admin_toggle_does_not_persist(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_auth
    ):
        mock_auth.is_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="user@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_admin_toggle_does_not_produce_audit_log(
        self, service, mock_audit, mock_auth
    ):
        mock_auth.is_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="user@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_audit.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_auth_service_is_queried_with_actor_identity(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_auth, existing_flag
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = existing_flag

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="dark_mode",
            environment="production",
            enabled=True,
        )

        mock_auth.is_admin.assert_called_once_with("admin@example.com")


# ===========================================================================
# 4. ENVIRONMENT VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestEnvironmentValidation:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "bad_env",
        ["prod", "PRODUCTION", "uat", "qa", "", "  ", "unknown"],
    )
    def test_unknown_environment_raises_error(self, service, bad_env):
        with pytest.raises(EnvironmentNotFoundError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment=bad_env,
                enabled=True,
            )

    def test_unknown_environment_does_not_persist(self, service, mock_repo):
        with pytest.raises(EnvironmentNotFoundError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment="uat",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_unknown_environment_does_not_produce_audit_log(
        self, service, mock_audit
    ):
        with pytest.raises(EnvironmentNotFoundError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="dark_mode",
                environment="uat",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_audit.log.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. FLAG NOT FOUND
# ===========================================================================

class TestFlagNotFound:

    def test_missing_flag_raises_error(self, service, mock_repo):
        mock_repo.get.side_effect = FeatureFlagNotFoundError("no such flag")

        with pytest.raises(FeatureFlagNotFoundError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="nonexistent_flag",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

    def test_missing_flag_does_not_persist(self, service, mock_repo):
        mock_repo.get.side_effect = FeatureFlagNotFoundError("no such flag")

        with pytest.raises(FeatureFlagNotFoundError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="nonexistent_flag",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_missing_flag_does_not_produce_audit_log(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit
    ):
        mock_repo.get.side_effect = FeatureFlagNotFoundError("no such flag")

        with pytest.raises(FeatureFlagNotFoundError):
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="nonexistent_flag",
                environment="production",
                enabled=True,
            )

        mock_audit.log.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 6. ISOLATION – flags are scoped per environment
# ===========================================================================

class TestPerEnvironmentIsolation:

    def test_toggle_in_staging_does_not_affect_production(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        staging_flag = FeatureFlag("feature_x", enabled=False)
        production_flag = FeatureFlag("feature_x", enabled=False)

        def _get(flag_name, environment):
            return staging_flag if environment == "staging" else production_flag

        mock_repo.get.side_effect = _get

        service.toggle(
            actor="admin@example.com",
            flag_name="feature_x",
            environment="staging",
            enabled=True,
        )

        # Only the staging flag should be mutated
        assert staging_flag.enabled is True
        assert production_flag.enabled is False

    def test_repository_save_receives_correct_environment_on_each_call(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = FeatureFlag("feature_x", enabled=False)

        for env in ("development", "staging", "production"):
            mock_repo.get.return_value = FeatureFlag("feature_x", enabled=False)
            service.toggle(
                actor="admin@example.com",
                flag_name="feature_x",
                environment=env,
                enabled=True,
            )

        saved_envs = [
            kw["environment"] for _, kw in mock_repo.save.call_args_list
        ]
        assert saved_envs == ["development", "staging", "production"]
```

---

## Test Coverage Summary

| Area | Test classes / key scenarios |
|---|---|
| **Happy path** | Enable/disable flag, correct return value, `save` called with right args, all 3 valid environments, idempotent toggle |
| **Audit logging** | Called exactly once, correct actor / flag / environment / previous & new values / action / UTC timestamp, logged *after* save, **not** logged when save raises |
| **Authorisation** | Non-admin raises `PermissionError`, no persist, no audit log; auth service queried with actor identity |
| **Environment validation** | 7 invalid environment strings raise `EnvironmentNotFoundError`; no persist/no audit |
| **Flag not found** | `FeatureFlagNotFoundError` propagated; no persist/no audit |
| **Per-env isolation** | Staging toggle doesn't mutate production object; `save` receives the right environment on every call |

### Key design decisions
* **Test doubles over real infra** – all I/O (DB, logger) is replaced with `MagicMock`, keeping tests fast and deterministic.  
* **Ordering assertion** – `test_audit_logged_after_successful_save` uses `side_effect` call-order tracking to enforce the *save-then-log* contract.  
* **`pytest.mark.parametrize`** – used for environment validity and invalid-environment strings to avoid duplication.  
* **Fixture composition** – small, single-responsibility fixtures (`mock_repo`, `mock_audit`, `mock_auth`, `service`, `existing_flag`) compose cleanly per test.

"""
Pytest tests for feature flag toggle functionality with audit logging.
Requirement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment;
             toggles shall be audit-logged.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake domain models (replace with real imports in production)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionError(Exception):
    pass


class EnvironmentNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class FeatureFlagNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class FeatureFlag:
    def __init__(self, name: str, enabled: bool = False):
        self.name = name
        self.enabled = enabled

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover
        return f"FeatureFlag(name={self.name!r}, enabled={self.enabled})"


class AuditLogEntry:
    def __init__(
        self,
        actor: str,
        action: str,
        flag_name: str,
        environment: str,
        previous_value: bool,
        new_value: bool,
        timestamp: datetime,
    ):
        self.actor = actor
        self.action = action
        self.flag_name = flag_name
        self.environment = environment
        self.previous_value = previous_value
        self.new_value = new_value
        self.timestamp = timestamp


class FeatureFlagService:
    """
    Service under test.  Uses injected repository and audit-logger so both
    can be replaced with test doubles.
    """

    VALID_ENVIRONMENTS = {"development", "staging", "production"}

    def __init__(self, repository: Any, audit_logger: Any, auth_service: Any):
        self._repo = repository
        self._audit = audit_logger
        self._auth = auth_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def toggle(
        self,
        *,
        actor: str,
        flag_name: str,
        environment: str,
        enabled: bool,
    ) -> FeatureFlag:
        # 1. Authorisation
        if not self._auth.is_admin(actor):
            raise PermissionError(f"{actor!r} is not an admin")

        # 2. Validate environment
        if environment not in self.VALID_ENVIRONMENTS:
            raise EnvironmentNotFoundError(f"Unknown environment: {environment!r}")

        # 3. Load existing flag (raises FeatureFlagNotFoundError if absent)
        flag = self._repo.get(flag_name=flag_name, environment=environment)

        previous_value = flag.enabled

        # 4. Persist the change
        flag.enabled = enabled
        self._repo.save(flag=flag, environment=environment)

        # 5. Audit log – MUST happen after a successful save
        self._audit.log(
            AuditLogEntry(
                actor=actor,
                action="TOGGLE",
                flag_name=flag_name,
                environment=environment,
                previous_value=previous_value,
                new_value=enabled,
                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            )
        )

        return flag


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_repo():
    return MagicMock(name="feature_flag_repository")


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_audit():
    return MagicMock(name="audit_logger")


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_auth():
    auth = MagicMock(name="auth_service")
    auth.is_admin.return_value = True          # default: actor IS an admin
    return auth


@pytest.fixture()
def service(mock_repo, mock_audit, mock_auth):
    return FeatureFlagService(
        repository=mock_repo,
        audit_logger=mock_audit,
        auth_service=mock_auth,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def existing_flag():
    """A flag that is currently disabled."""
    return FeatureFlag(name="dark_mode", enabled=False)


# -----------------
